titleStmt.title: SII 11.25: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 25. (B.K. No. 60 of 1926-27). YALI-SIRŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A HERO-STONE SET UP NEAR THE VILLAGE chāvaḍi.

summary.p: This incomplete inscription which is dated in Śaka 823, Durmati (= A.D. 901) belongs to the reign of Akālavarsha (i.e., Kṛishṇa II) and mentions his subordinate Mahā-Śrīmanta as ruling over the Beḷvala Three Hundred province. This chief is identical with Mahā-Sirivanta of the Veṅkaṭāpūr inscription dated in Śaka 828 (= A.D. 907) (No. 28 below).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti śakanṛpakālātīta saṁvatsarasataṅgaḷeṇṭunūṟa Irpvatta mūṟaneya dhurmmati yemba saṁvatsaraṁ pravarttiThetsa is engraved below the line.
sa svastyakaḷavariṣa śrīpṛthuvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēsvara para ma bhaṭṭaraka ruttarōttaThe letter ra is engraved below the line.
rābhivṛddhi pravarddhamāna vijayerājyaṁgiyye mahāśrīmanta beḷvelamūnūṟuma nāḷuttiraThe inscription stops
Digital edition of SII 11.25 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
